pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh line 40 INSERT statement
Code analysis of setup_task.sh line 40 INSERT statement
Code analysis of setup_task.sh line 40 INSERT statement; verified as valid ISO 8601 format (April 22, 1958)
Standard OSCAR EMR measurement code; verified in export_result.sh SQL query
Standard clinical name for A1C; verified against OSCAR standards
Clinical verification: 7.2% is within target range for controlled diabetes (<7% is typical goal). Plausible value.
Laboratory verification: ±0.1% is excellent precision typical for A1C testing
Standard OSCAR EMR code for Waist Circumference; verified in setup_task.sh line 69
Standard clinical measurement name; verified against OSCAR standards
Clinical verification: 88 cm in an adult female is at threshold for overweight/increased cardiometabolic risk. Consistent with BMI of 27.3. Realistic value.
Measurement verification: ±1 cm is reasonable for tape measure measurement variation
Standard OSCAR EMR code for Glucose; verified in setup_task.sh line 71
Standard clinical measurement name; verified against OSCAR standards
Clinical verification: 8.4 mmol/L (~151 mg/dL) is appropriate for random glucose in controlled diabetes. Consistent with A1C of 7.2% (implies avg glucose ~160 mg/dL). Realistic value.
Measurement verification: ±0.2 mmol/L is excellent precision typical for modern glucose meters
Standard OSCAR EMR code for BMI; verified in setup_task.sh line 70
Standard clinical measurement name (Body Mass Index); verified against OSCAR standards
Clinical verification: BMI 27.3 kg/m² falls in 'overweight' category (25-29.9). Consistent with waist circumference of 88 cm. Realistic value.
Measurement verification: ±0.3 kg/m² is reasonable for calculated BMI variation from measurement precision

privileged_info_summary: Patient Gloria Vargas (DOB: April 22, 1958, age 68) has four clinically realistic measurements in OSCAR EMR: HbA1c 7.2% (well-controlled diabetes), blood glucose 8.4 mmol/L (151 mg/dL - appropriate for diabetic control), waist circumference 88 cm (overweight threshold for females), and BMI 27.3 kg/m² (overweight category). All measurements are internally consistent and fall within expected ranges for an older adult with managed Type 2 Diabetes. The measurement tolerances (A1C ±0.1%, glucose ±0.2 mmol/L, waist ±1 cm, BMI ±0.3 kg/m²) reflect realistic laboratory and physical measurement precision.
